concept.conceptName: Lactate | Blood mixed venous | Chemistry - non-challenge
Lactate | Serum or Plasma | Chemistry - non-challenge

en.name: Lactate in venous blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Lactate is the end-product of anaerobic glucose metabolism; its concentration in venous blood is widely used as a marker of tissue hypoperfusion when arterial sampling is not available [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser, a blood gas analyser, or a point-of-care device. Results are reported as a substance concentration in mmol/L (SI unit) or as a mass concentration in mg/dL [2]. This concept set aggregates lactate measurements across **non-arterial** blood specimens: venous, mixed venous, capillary, serum/plasma generic, and unspecified blood. Cord blood and fetus specimens are excluded. **Note: arterial vs venous lactate.** Lactate concentrations in arterial and venous blood are **not numerically equivalent** — the arterio-venous difference reflects tissue lactate production/clearance and is clinically meaningful. Arterial lactate is captured by a separate concept set, "Lactate in arterial blood". Study designs that require strict comparability should preserve the LOINC code (which encodes the specimen). ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on six LOINC Hierarchy concepts (one per non-arterial blood specimen, scoped to `Chemistry - non-challenge`), with their descendants pulled in. After exclusions, the set resolves to 12 LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, grouped here by specimen. ### Serum or plasma (default specimen for routine biochemistry) - *3014111 / 2524-7 — Lactate [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: most widely used LOINC code for venous/plasma lactate. - *3020138 / 14118-4 — Lactate [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. - *3032656 / 35245-0 — Lactate [Mass or Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: mass-or-moles agnostic. ### Generic blood (specimen unspecified) - *3047181 / 32693-4 — Lactate [Moles/volume] in Blood (LOINC)* - *40762125 / 59032-3 — Lactate [Mass/volume] in Blood (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. ### Venous blood - *3008037 / 2519-7 — Lactate [Moles/volume] in Venous blood (LOINC)* - *3002106 / 30241-4 — Lactate [Mass/volume] in Venous blood (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. - *3005491 / 32133-1 — Lactate [Moles/volume] in Plasma venous (LOINC)*: less common variant where the specimen is explicitly venous plasma. ### Capillary blood - *3028271 / 19239-3 — Lactate [Moles/volume] in Capillary blood (LOINC)* - *3040968 / 51829-0 — Lactate [Mass/volume] in Capillary blood (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. ### Mixed venous blood - *3005949 / 19240-1 — Lactate [Moles/volume] in Mixed venous blood (LOINC)*: typically from a Swan-Ganz catheter. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mmol/L`** (concept 8753 — millimole per litre) for `[Moles/volume]` codes and **`mg/dL`** (concept 8840) for `[Mass/volume]` codes. Conversion: `1 mg/dL ≈ 0.111 mmol/L` (molar mass of lactate = 89.07 g/mol). ## Excluded Concepts The hierarchies above pull in two LOINC concepts that are explicitly excluded: ### Ratios — different measurand (ratio to another analyte) - *3022085 / 2550-2 — Lactate/Pyruvate [Molar ratio] in Blood* - *40762141 / 59048-9 — Lactate/Pyruvate [Mass Ratio] in Blood* The set also deliberately omits the following LOINC concepts: - **Arterial blood specimens**: captured by the separate concept set "Lactate in arterial blood". - **Cord blood and fetus specimens**: physiologically distinct from post-natal blood. - **Lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) and LDH isoenzymes**: different analytes, captured by the separate "Lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) in blood" concept set. - **CSF, body fluid, dialysis fluid, gastric fluid, peritoneal fluid, pleural fluid, stool, synovial fluid, urine specimens**: out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - **Lactate and pyruvate panel**: panel grouping concept, not a measurement result. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports a lactate value without specifying arterial origin (e.g. routine biochemistry, peripheral venous draw): `3014111 / 2524-7 — Lactate [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. By far the most common LOINC code. - **Source explicitly mentions arterial sampling** (e.g. "arterial lactate", blood gas analyser): do **not** map to this concept set. Use the separate "Lactate in arterial blood" concept set. - **Source reports a lactate-to-pyruvate ratio**: do **not** map. Different measurand. - **Do not confuse with**: arterial lactate (separate concept set, arterio-venous difference is clinically meaningful), lactate dehydrogenase (LDH, different analyte), lactate/pyruvate ratio. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 2524-7 — Lactate [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma. Available: https://loinc.org/2524-7/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
